followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS review ne ek baar bachaya: developer ne BSEG table use karna plan kiya tha large join ke saath — reviewer ne suggest kiya ki BSIK (open items specific) zyada efficient hai. TS mein fix kiya — code mein hota toh refactoring ka kaam hota.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS mein logic flow skip karna 'main code mein dekh lunga' sochke — design decisions coding se pehle honi chahiye. TS review hi woh checkpoint hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS document sections:
" 1. Object: ZRFI_VENDOR_AGING (Report, FI module)
" 2. Tables: LFB1, BSIK, T001
" 3. Selection Screen: BUKRS(mandatory), LIFNR range, BLDAT range
" 4. Logic:
"    a. Validate BUKRS in T001
"    b. AUTHORITY-CHECK F_BKPF_BUK
"    c. SELECT LFB1 where bukrs IN s_bukrs
"    d. FOR ALL ENTRIES: SELECT BSIK
"    e. Calculate aging bucket
"    f. OO ALV display with subtotals

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you identify correct SAP tables from a Functional Specification?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Table identification FS mein business object se shuru hota hai. Vendor requirements ke liye: LFA1 (general master), LFB1 (company code data), BSIK (open items), BSAK (cleared items). Sales ke liye: orders ke liye VBAK/VBAP, document flow ke liye VBFA, billing ke liye VBRK/VBRP. FI ke liye: ECC mein BKPF/BSEG, S/4HANA mein ACDOCA. Main relevant standard transaction pe ST05 SQL trace bhi use karta hoon dekhne ke liye ki same business process ke liye SAP khud kaunse tables read karta hai — yeh most reliable table discovery technique hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Table identification starts with the business object in the FS. For vendor requirements: LFA1 (general master), LFB1 (company code data), BSIK (open items), BSAK (cleared items). For sales: VBAK/VBAP for orders, VBFA for document flow, VBRK/VBRP for billing. For FI: BKPF/BSEG in ECC, ACDOCA in S/4HANA. I also use ST05 SQL trace on the relevant standard transaction to see which tables SAP itself reads for the same business process — this is the most reliable table discovery technique.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS mein 'vendor payment status' tha. Correct table unclear tha. FB60 transaction ST05 trace chalaya — BSIK (open), BSAK (cleared), REGUP (payment run) tables hit hue. TS mein correctly documented, code right first time.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Tables guess karna bina verification ke. Wrong table = wrong data = wrong report. Always verify with SE16N actual data before committing to TS.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Table discovery via ST05:
" 1. ST05 -> Activate trace
" 2. Open standard transaction (e.g., FBL1N for vendor items)
" 3. Execute with sample vendor
" 4. ST05 -> Deactivate -> Display trace
" 5. Tables accessed: LFA1, LFB1, BSIK, BSEG...
" 6. Document in TS: These are the correct tables

followupAnswerBank.question: What validation rules from FS do you implement in ABAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: FS validation rules directly ABAP implementation pe map karte hain: mandatory fields SELECT-OPTIONS ya PARAMETERS pe OBLIGATORY addition use karte hain. Range validations — 'date range max 1 year' — AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein date parameters ke beech difference check karke code hote hain. Valid value checks — 'company code must exist' — T001 jaise master tables se SELECT SINGLE use karte hain. Cross-field validations parameters compare karte hain aur FS se exact error text ke saath processing halt karne ke liye MESSAGE TYPE 'E' raise karte hain. AT SELECTION-SCREEN processing begin hone se pehle saare input validations ke liye correct event hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: FS validation rules map directly to ABAP implementation: mandatory fields use OBLIGATORY addition on SELECT-OPTIONS or PARAMETERS. Range validations — 'date range max 1 year' — are coded in AT SELECTION-SCREEN checking the difference between date parameters. Valid value checks — 'company code must exist' — use SELECT SINGLE from master tables like T001. Cross-field validations compare parameters and raise MESSAGE TYPE 'E' to halt processing with the exact error text from the FS. AT SELECTION-SCREEN is the correct event for all input validations before processing begins.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: FS said: 'If both customer and vendor ranges are empty, show error.' Developer implemented this in START-OF-SELECTION — should have been AT SELECTION-SCREEN. User clicked Execute, waited 2 minutes, then got error. Fix: move validation to AT SELECTION-SCREEN — immediate feedback.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Input validation START-OF-SELECTION mein karna — user wait karta hai before error. Always AT SELECTION-SCREEN for input checks — immediate response.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " FS: 'Company code is mandatory'
  IF s_bukrs IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'Company code is required' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.
  " FS: 'Date range max 1 year'
  IF p_dateto - p_datefr > 365.
    MESSAGE 'Date range cannot exceed 1 year' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What should the logic flow section of a TS look like?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS ka logic flow section program ka ek numbered pseudocode walkthrough hai — detailed enough taaki dusra developer ise code kar sake bina design decisions banaye. Yeh include karta hai: har major step accessed table aur WHERE condition ke saath, empty data ya errors ke liye branching, har step pe exception handling, subroutine ya method organization, aur output building logic. Yeh ek senior developer dwara review hota hai jo ek single line of code likhne se pehle wrong tables, missing SY-SUBRC checks, ya performance anti-patterns jaise design issues catch kar sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The logic flow section of a TS is a numbered pseudocode walkthrough of the program — detailed enough that another developer could code it without needing to make design decisions. It includes: each major step with the table accessed and WHERE condition, branching for empty data or errors, exception handling at each step, subroutine or method organization, and output building logic. This is reviewed by a senior developer who can catch design issues like wrong tables, missing SY-SUBRC checks, or performance anti-patterns before a single line of code is written.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS logic review ne ek baar design flaw pakda: developer FOR ALL ENTRIES plan kar raha tha vendor table se BSIK fetch ke liye — reviewer ne bataya ki BSIK already vendor key rakhta hai, direct WHERE clause better hai. TS mein change, code first-time-right.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Logic flow mein sirf high-level steps likhna 'fetch data, process, display' — yeh TS nahi, outline hai. TS mein enough detail honi chahiye ki reviewer technical correctness check kar sake.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Logic Flow (pseudocode):
" 1. AT SEL-SCREEN: Validate BUKRS in T001 (SE=0 else E)
" 2. AUTH-CHECK: F_BKPF_BUK / BUKRS=s_bukrs / ACTVT=03
" 3. SELECT LFB1: WHERE bukrs IN s_bukrs -> lt_vendors
"    IF empty: Message 'No vendors found' TYPE I, EXIT
" 4. FOR ALL ENTRIES lt_vendors:
"    SELECT BSIK: WHERE lifnr=lt_vendors-lifnr -> lt_items
"    IF empty: Message 'No open items' TYPE I, EXIT
" 5. LOOP lt_items: calculate lv_days = sy-datum - bldat
"                  assign aging bucket CASE lv_days
" 6. Summarize: COLLECT into lt_summary by lifnr+bucket
" 7. OO ALV display lt_summary with subtotals

followupAnswerBank.question: What test cases should be in the Technical Specification?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Technical Specification test cases cover karne chahiye: expected output produce karne wala valid data ke saath positive scenario, appropriate message produce karne wala no matching data ke saath negative scenario, boundary conditions (maximum date range, empty selection), authorization failure (required object ke bina user ko error mile, data nahi), agreed time ke andar maximum realistic dataset ke saath performance test, aur error handling (invalid company code, system errors). Har test case mein inputs, expected output, aur pass criteria include hote hain. Yeh test cases developer unit test guidance aur QA test planning input dono ke roop mein serve karte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Technical Specification test cases should cover: positive scenario with valid data producing expected output, negative scenario with no matching data producing appropriate message, boundary conditions (maximum date range, empty selection), authorization failure (user without required object gets error, not data), performance test with maximum realistic dataset within agreed time, and error handling (invalid company code, system errors). Each test case includes inputs, expected output, and pass criteria. These test cases serve both as developer unit test guidance and QA test planning input.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS mein auth test case miss kiya. QA ne test kiya authorized user se — pass. Production pe unauthorized user ne data dekh liya — security audit finding. Lesson: always include negative auth test case in TS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf happy-path test case likhna. Negative, boundary, aur security test cases equally important hain — production issues mostly edge cases mein hote hain.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Test Cases:
" TC-01 (Positive): BUKRS=1000, LIFNR=L001-L999, BLDAT=Jan-Mar
"   Expected: 250 vendor rows, 3 aging buckets, correct totals

" TC-02 (Negative): BUKRS=1000, LIFNR=ZZZ (non-existent)
"   Expected: 'No open items found' message, no data

" TC-03 (Auth): User with no F_BKPF_BUK for 1000
"   Expected: Authorization error, no data displayed

" TC-04 (Performance): BUKRS=1000, all vendors, full year
"   Expected: Completes in < 3 minutes

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you document transport information in a Technical Specification?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek TS ka transport section document karta hai: TS approval ke baad created workbench transport request number, saare included ABAP objects (program, includes, message class, data elements), dependent objects aur unke transport requests, transport route aur target systems, project schedule ke saath aligned planned import dates, configuration transports jo is change ke saath accompany karne chahiye, aur import sequence. Yeh ensure karta hai ki go-live time pe koi object forgotten na ho aur basis team ke pas ek clear import plan ho. Object list grow hone ke saath development ke throughout transport information update hoti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The transport section of a TS documents: the workbench transport request number created after TS approval, all ABAP objects included (program, includes, message class, data elements), dependent objects and their transport requests, the transport route and target systems, planned import dates aligned with project schedule, configuration transports that must accompany this change, and the import sequence. This ensures no object is forgotten at go-live time and the basis team has a clear import plan. Transport information is updated throughout development as the object list grows.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live day: transport missed 2 dependent objects — DTEL and MSAG. TS transport section unhe list nahi karta tha. Emergency additional transports. Lesson: maintain live transport list in TS throughout development, not just at start.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Transport section TS mein once fill karna aur update nahi karna — as development progresses, new objects add hote hain. Transport list dynamically update karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Transport Section:
" Primary request: DEVK900045
"   R3TR PROG ZRFI_VENDOR_AGING
"   R3TR MSAG ZRFI_VENDOR_MSG
" Dependent requests:
"   DEVK900040: R3TR DTEL ZVENDOR_AGING_DAYS (data element)
"   DEVK900041: R3TR DOMA ZAGING_BUCKET (domain)
" Import sequence: DEVK900040 -> DEVK900041 -> DEVK900045
" Planned QA import: 2026-06-10
" Planned PRD import: 2026-06-20

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the peer review process for Technical Specifications?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS peer review mein ek senior developer ya tech lead coding begin hone se pehle complete TS review karta hai. Reviewer check karta hai: correct table aur field identification, flow ki logical completeness, har step pe proper exception handling, authorization objects aur values, performance approach appropriateness (join vs FAE vs CDS), naming convention compliance, aur sufficient test case coverage. Review comments developer dwara addressed hote hain aur reviewer re-approve karta hai. Yeh gate design-level defects prevent karta hai jo coding, testing, ya — worst — production deployment ke baad fix karne ke liye exponentially more expensive hote hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TS peer review involves a senior developer or tech lead reviewing the complete TS before coding begins. The reviewer checks: correct table and field identification, logical completeness of the flow, proper exception handling at every step, authorization objects and values, performance approach appropriateness (join vs FAE vs CDS), naming convention compliance, and sufficient test case coverage. Review comments are addressed by the developer and the reviewer re-approves. This gate prevents design-level defects which are exponentially more expensive to fix after coding, testing, or — worst — after production deployment.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS review saved a project: developer used BKPF table which in S/4HANA is a compatibility view — reviewer caught it, suggested ACDOCA. If coded and transported — performance issue and potential data inconsistency in production. TS review = cheapest quality gate.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: TS review skip karna time pressure mein. 'Main senior developer hun, review ki zaroorat nahi' — wrong. Even senior developers benefit from a second pair of eyes, especially for S/4HANA considerations.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Peer Review Checklist:
" [ ] Tables verified in SE11 and SE16N with real data
" [ ] Logic covers all FS scenarios
" [ ] SY-SUBRC handled at every DB call step
" [ ] AUTHORITY-CHECK included per FS auth requirement
" [ ] Performance: No SELECT inside LOOP plan
" [ ] S/4HANA: No deprecated tables (BKPF direct?)
" [ ] Test cases: Positive, negative, auth, performance
" [ ] Naming convention: Z prefix, module code, description
